description:
You are a DevSecOps engineer who has just been paged because two application-side
INI configuration files drifted from corporate policy.  
Your mission is to:

1. Locate every “*.ini” file under  
   /home/user/policy/configs  
   (there are exactly two files, app1.ini and app2.ini).

2. Assess each file against the following **mandatory policy**:
   a. In section [security] the key enforce_tls **must** be present and set to  
      “true” (case-insensitive).  
   b. In section [logging] the key level **must** be present and set to  
      “INFO” or the more restrictive “WARN”, “ERROR”, “FATAL”.  
      (Anything more verbose than INFO, e.g. DEBUG, TRACE, is non-compliant.)

3. Remediate every non-compliant setting **in-place** while keeping formatting
   and comments intact:
   • If a required key is missing, add it.  
   • If a required key exists with the wrong value, replace the value.  
   • Do **not** alter already-compliant keys.  
   • Create a **timestamp-free** backup of each file you modify by appending the
     extension “.bak” in the same directory (e.g. app1.ini.bak).  
     Do not generate backup files for already-compliant INIs.

4. Produce a single human-readable remediation log at  
   /home/user/policy/reports/after_remediation.log  
   The file must be created (and overwritten if it exists) by your workflow and
   follow **exactly** this format so that automated tests can parse it:

   FILE: <absolute-path-to-ini>
   SECURITY_ENFORCED: was=<value-before> -> <value-after>
   LOG_LEVEL:          was=<value-before> -> <value-after>
   STATUS: <REMEDIATED|NO_ACTION_REQUIRED>
   ----

   …repeat the four-line block for every scanned INI…
   (four dashes “----” as a delimiter, no extra blank lines)

   After the last block, append this three-line summary (exact spelling,
   upper-case, and order are mandatory):

   Summary:
   FILES_SCANNED=<N>
   REMEDIATED=<M>
   COMPLIANT=<K>

   where N is the total INI count, M is the number that needed changes,
   and K is the number that were already fully compliant.

5. Use parallel shell capabilities (e.g., background “&” jobs, xargs –P, GNU
   parallel, or any safe equivalent) so that compliance checks on different
   INIs occur concurrently.  Make sure all background work has finished before
   producing the log file—otherwise the counts will be wrong.

Hints you may consider (not mandatory):
• awk, grep, or crudini can help with INI parsing, but plain POSIX tools are
  acceptable.  
• Keep file permissions unchanged except for adding write bits that you
  actually need.

When you are done, “cat” the content of
/home/user/policy/reports/after_remediation.log
to the console so reviewers can quickly see the result.
truth:
Initial file layout before the agent starts:

/home/user/policy/configs/app1.ini
------------------------------------------------
[security]
enforce_tls=false

[logging]
level=DEBUG
------------------------------------------------

/home/user/policy/configs/app2.ini
------------------------------------------------
[security]
enforce_tls=true

[logging]
level=INFO
------------------------------------------------

/home/user/policy/reports/    (exists and is writable, empty)
/home/user/policy/configs/    (contains the two INIs above, writable)

Expected state **after** successful task execution:

1. /home/user/policy/configs/app1.ini.bak must exist and contain the exact
   original text shown above.

2. /home/user/policy/configs/app1.ini must now read:
------------------------------------------------
[security]
enforce_tls=true

[logging]
level=INFO
------------------------------------------------

3. /home/user/policy/configs/app2.ini is unchanged and therefore **no**
   .bak file for it must be present.

4. /home/user/policy/reports/after_remediation.log must exist with precisely:
------------------------------------------------
FILE: /home/user/policy/configs/app1.ini
SECURITY_ENFORCED: was=false -> true
LOG_LEVEL:          was=DEBUG -> INFO
STATUS: REMEDIATED
----
FILE: /home/user/policy/configs/app2.ini
SECURITY_ENFORCED: was=true -> true
LOG_LEVEL:          was=INFO -> INFO
STATUS: NO_ACTION_REQUIRED
----
Summary:
FILES_SCANNED=2
REMEDIATED=1
COMPLIANT=1
------------------------------------------------
(no trailing spaces, each line ends with a single newline,
exactly four dashes in the delimiter lines)

Automated tests will verify:
• Presence and content of app1.ini.bak  
• Post-remediation content of both INIs  
• Absence of app2.ini.bak  
• Exact content and path of after_remediation.log  
• Correct counts in the Summary block  
• That at least one subshell/background job was used (checked via “ps –ef”
  timing and shell history inspection).

Permissions:
All files and directories already belong to user “user” with mode 0644 for
files and 0755 for directories, so the agent can read and write without sudo.
name: task_000000_ebbea6a6
